item.title: 【When Retries Become a Denial-of-Wallet】当重试变成钱包被拒绝时
item.description:
【

There's a particular kind of incident that doesn't show up in your error dashboards. No alerts fire. Latency looks fine, actually — or fine-ish, in that flickering, indeterminate way that makes you suspicious but not certain. What shows up, days later, is a billing anomaly. A line item that's 4x what you budgeted. And when you dig, you find it: retries. Hundreds of thousands of them. Loyal, tireless, utterly pointless retries, hammering a dependency that was never going to recover within the retry window, each one spinning up a Lambda invocation, writing to CloudWatch, touching the database, accruing egress. The system was "retrying" its way into insolvency.

This is what I mean when I call uncontrolled retries a self-inflicted Denial-of-Wallet attack. Not metaphorically. Mechanically.

】

有一种特殊的事件不会显示在您的错误仪表板中。没有警报火灾。事实上，延迟看起来不错——或者说还不错，以那种闪烁的、不确定的方式让你怀疑但又不确定。几天后出现的情况是计费异常。订单项的预算是您预算的 4 倍。当你挖掘时，你会发现它：重试。其中有数十万。忠诚、不知疲倦、完全毫无意义的重试，敲打在重试窗口内永远无法恢复的依赖项，每一次都启动 Lambda 调用、写入 CloudWatch、接触数据库、累积出口。该系统正在“重试”破产。

这就是我将不受控制的重试称为自我造成的拒绝钱包攻击的意思。不是比喻意义上的。机械地。

item.title: 【ARC: The Architecture for Reasoning Control】ARC：推理控制架构
item.description:
【

Three Lessons from an AI Makeathon

I recently participated in a makeathon focused on building AI-powered applications. Over 2–3 intense days, I watched teams go from idea to demo — and the patterns that separated working products from frustrated debugging sessions were remarkably consistent, especially for teams building AI agents.

From this makeathon and from my experience working with teams building AI applications and agents, here are the three lessons I took away on how to build reliable AI applications by engineering around non-determinism. Together, these form what I would like to call “The Architecture for Reasoning Control”.

】

AI Makeathon 的三个教训

我最近参加了一场专注于构建人工智能应用程序的制作马拉松。在紧张的 2-3 天里，我观察了团队从想法到演示的过程，并且将工作产品与受挫的调试会话区分开的模式非常一致，特别是对于构建 AI 代理的团队而言。

通过这次 makeathon 以及我与构建 AI 应用程序和代理的团队合作的经验，以下是我学到的关于如何通过围绕非确定性进行工程来构建可靠的 AI 应用程序的三个经验教训。这些共同构成了我所说的“推理控制架构”。

item.title: 【Designing Agentic Systems Like Distributed Systems】像分布式系统一样设计代理系统
item.description:
【

Agentic development is rapidly becoming one of the most talked-about paradigms in software development. The talk is not just of using AI to assist in coding but of using systems where an AI agent is capable of planning, executing tasks, and even deciding.

From a surface-level perspective, agentic systems are a new abstraction. But if we look under the hood, we find something that looks rather familiar: distributed systems.

】

代理开发正在迅速成为软件开发中最受关注的范例之一。这次演讲不仅涉及使用人工智能辅助编码，还涉及使用人工智能代理能够规划、执行任务甚至决策的系统。

从表面的角度来看，代理系统是一个新的抽象。但如果我们深入研究，我们会发现一些看起来相当熟悉的东西：分布式系统。

item.title: 【The Hidden Failure Modes of AI Systems (That Traditional Monitoring Misses)】人工智能系统的隐藏故障模式（传统监控遗漏的）
item.description:
【

One of the most unsettling characteristics of AI systems is how often they appear perfectly healthy. Infrastructure dashboards report stable CPU utilization, normal latency levels, and acceptable throughput. No alerts are triggered. From an operational standpoint, the system is functioning exactly as designed.

Yet the outputs are wrong. In many AI deployments, engineers eventually encounter this situation: a recommendation system begins suggesting irrelevant items, a support chatbot produces inconsistent answers, or an AI assistant gradually becomes less reliable in answering domain-specific questions. 

】

人工智能系统最令人不安的特征之一是它们经常表现得非常健康。基础设施仪表板报告稳定的 CPU 利用率、正常的延迟水平和可接受的吞吐量。不会触发任何警报。从操作的角度来看，系统完全按照设计运行。

但是输出是错误的。在许多AI部署中，工程师最终都会遇到这种情况：推荐系统开始建议不相关的项目，支持聊天机器人产生不一致的答案，或者AI助手在回答特定领域的问题时逐渐变得不太可靠。

item.title: 【Top JavaScript/TypeScript Gen AI Frameworks for 2026】2026 年顶级 JavaScript/TypeScript Gen AI 框架
item.description:
【

The generative AI tooling ecosystem has exploded over the past two years. What started as a handful of Python libraries has grown into a rich, opinionated landscape of frameworks spanning multiple languages, deployment targets, and philosophical bets. As a developer who has shipped production applications using all five of the frameworks covered in this article, Genkit, Vercel AI SDK, Mastra, LangChain, and Google ADK, I want to offer a practical, hands-on view of where each one excels, where each one falls short, and what I would reach for depending on the project I’m building.

This is not a benchmark post. Tokens per second and latency numbers go stale within weeks. Instead, this is a developer experience and architecture comparison, the kind of thing that matters when you’re deciding what framework will carry your product through 2026 and beyond.

】

生成式 AI 工具生态系统在过去两年中呈爆炸式增长。最初的几个 Python 库已经发展成为一个丰富的、固执己见的框架，涵盖多种语言、部署目标和哲学赌注。作为一名使用本文介绍的所有五个框架（Genkit、Vercel AI SDK、Mastra、LangChain 和 Google ADK）发布生产应用程序的开发人员，我想提供一个实用的实践视图，了解每个框架的优点、不足以及我将根据我正在构建的项目实现的目标。

这不是基准帖子。每秒的令牌数和延迟数在几周内就会过时。相反，这是开发者体验和架构比较，当您决定哪种框架将支撑您的产品度过 2026 年及以后时，这一点很重要。

item.title: 【Cost-Aware Routing for RAG: Fetch Less, Spend Less, Answer Better】RAG 的成本感知路由：获取更少、花费更少、响应更好
item.description:
【

You have a knowledge base full of PDFs. Someone asks: "What do you know about RAG?" Your RAG system dutifully searches all the documents, retrieves 10 passages, stuffs them into the prompt, and generates the answer. The problem? The LLM already knew the answer. You just paid for 10 passages you did not need.

This is the silent tax of static RAG — and most teams do not realize they are paying it on every single query.

】

您有一个充满 PDF 的知识库。有人问：“您对 RAG 了解多少？”您的 RAG 系统会尽职尽责地搜索所有文档，检索 10 个段落，将它们填充到提示中，然后生成答案。问题？ LLM 已经知道答案了。您刚刚支付了 10 个不需要的段落费用。

这是静态 RAG 的无声税 - 大多数团队没有意识到他们正在为每个查询付出代价。

item.title: 【Why PostgreSQL CDC Breaks in Production】为什么 PostgreSQL CDC 在生产中中断
item.description:
【

Keeping two PostgreSQL databases in sync sounds simple. Until it isn’t.

At first, everything looks fine:

】

保持两个 PostgreSQL 数据库同步听起来很简单。直到它不再存在为止。

起初，一切看起来都很好：

item.title: 【The Rise of AI Orchestrators】人工智能协调器的崛起
item.description:
【

“They have not written a single line of code since December,” and now “only generate code and supervise it.” That is how Spotify co-CEO Gustav Söderström described some of the company’s most senior engineers on Spotify’s Q4 2025 earnings call. He added that the change is “real” and “happening fast.”

That remark matters not because it is provocative, but because it points to a broader shift in engineering work. As code generation becomes faster and cheaper, the differentiator moves upward: toward framing the problem correctly, setting constraints, reviewing outcomes critically, and deciding what is actually ready to ship. Value does not disappear; it relocates from raw production to direction, judgment, and accountability.

】

“自 12 月以来，他们没有写过一行代码”，现在“只生成代码并对其进行监督”。 Spotify 联合首席执行官 Gustav Söderström 在 Spotify 2025 年第四季度财报电话会议上如此描述该公司的一些最高级工程师。他补充说，这种变化是“真实的”并且“发生得很快”。

这句话很重要，不是因为它具有挑衅性，而是因为它指出了工程工作中更广泛的转变。随着代码生成变得更快、更便宜，差异化因素就会向上移动：正确地构建问题、设置约束、批判性地审查结果，并决定实际准备交付什么。价值不会消失；它从原始生产转向指导、判断和问责。

item.title: 【AI in Software Engineering: 3 Critical Mistakes to Avoid (and What to Do Instead)】软件工程中的人工智能：要避免的 3 个关键错误（以及该怎么做）
item.description:
【

The term tool comes from the Old English tōl, meaning an instrument to achieve a purpose. Historically, every major shift in engineering — from the steam engine of the Industrial Revolution to the rise of modern computing — has followed the same pattern: tools amplify human capability, but they do not replace judgment. Artificial intelligence is no exception. It promises acceleration, abstraction, and productivity, yet it also carries a familiar risk: when misunderstood or misapplied, it scales mistakes just as efficiently as it scales success.

In software engineering, this duality becomes even more evident. AI can generate code, suggest architectures, and even simulate reasoning, but it operates on probabilistic patterns derived from existing codebases — many of which reflect average, not excellent, engineering practices. The result is subtle: systems that work, but are fragile, inconsistent, or difficult to evolve. This is not a failure of AI itself, but of how we, as engineers, frame and guide its use. Much like early adoption of frameworks or cloud computing, the real challenge is not the technology — it is the discipline required to use it well.

】

术语工具源自古英语tōl，意思是实现目的的工具。从历史上看，工程领域的每一次重大转变——从工业革命的蒸汽机到现代计算的兴起——都遵循相同的模式：工具增强了人类的能力，但它们并不能取代判断力。 人工智能也不例外。它承诺加速、抽象和生产力，但它也带来了一个熟悉的风险：当被误解或误用时，它会像扩大成功一样有效地扩大错误。

在软件工程中，这种二元性变得更加明显。人工智能可以生成代码、建议架构，甚至模拟推理，但它运行在从现有代码库派生的概率模式上——其中许多代码反映了平均而非优秀的工程实践。结果是微妙的：系统工作，但脆弱、不一致或难以发展。这并不是人工智能本身的失败，而是我们作为工程师如何构建和指导其使用的失败。就像早期采用框架或云计算一样，真正的挑战不是技术，而是良好使用技术所需的纪律。

item.title: 【Difference Between EnvironmentObject, StateObject, ObservedObject and Observable】EnvironmentObject、StateObject、ObservedObject 和 Observable 之间的区别
item.description:
【

@StateObject, @EnvironmentObject, and @ObservedObject

I’ve decided to dedicate this week to exploring data flow in SwiftUI. In this article, we’ll discuss the differences between the @StateObject, @EnvironmentObject, and @ObservedObject property wrappers. From my experience, this is often the most confusing topic for developers just starting out with SwiftUI.

Why do we need property wrappers in SwiftUI?

SwiftUI uses immutable struct types to describe the view hierarchy. Every view provided by the framework is inherently immutable. This is why SwiftUI provides a specific set of property wrappers to handle data changes.

】

@StateObject、@EnvironmentObject 和 @ObservedObject

我决定本周专门探索SwiftUI 中的数据流。在本文中，我们将讨论 @StateObject、@EnvironmentObject 和 @ObservedObject 属性包装器之间的差异。根据我的经验，对于刚开始使用 SwiftUI 的开发人员来说，这通常是最令人困惑的主题。

为什么我们需要 SwiftUI 中的属性包装器？

SwiftUI 使用不可变结构类型来描述视图层次结构。框架提供的每个视图本质上都是不可变的。这就是为什么 SwiftUI 提供了一组特定的属性包装器来处理数据更改。
